findings.description: Page 1 PDF has a clear two-column layout for county data (left and right county groups side by side). The HTML conversion preserves this as a table but renders it in a different visual structure with alternating rows rather than side-by-side columns.
findings.evidence: PDF shows two distinct column groups (e.g., Alamance/Macon on same row). HTML shows the same data in a single wide table that appears to maintain the two-column structure, though with different styling.

findings.description: Table 1 in HTML uses a simplified table format compared to the PDF. The data appears complete but horizontal rules and grouping separators between county groups are not preserved.
findings.evidence: PDF has subtle spacing/line breaks between county groups (e.g., between Brunswick and Buncombe). HTML renders as continuous rows without these visual groupings.

findings.description: Table 2 in the PDF is rendered as a two-column layout with business types on left and right. The HTML version breaks this into a single long scrolling table.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 shows left column (3% Tax Group, Apparel, Automotive, Food, Furniture, General Merchandise) and right column (Lumber & Building, Unclassified Group, Total, Combined General Rate, etc.) side by side. HTML renders all business types sequentially in one column.

findings.description: Table 2 in HTML is split across multiple separate tables rather than maintaining the unified two-column table structure of the PDF. The left-side categories and right-side categories that were visually paired are now separated into sequential sections.
findings.evidence: PDF shows Type of Business columns side by side with their respective Gross Collections and Taxable Sales. HTML separates left and right into a long sequential list.

findings.description: Group headers in Table 2 (e.g., 'Apparel Group - 4.75%', 'Automotive Group - 4.75%') appear bold in the PDF but the HTML rendering shows them with less visual distinction from sub-items.
findings.evidence: PDF has bold group headers with dollar amounts. HTML shows group headers but formatting emphasis is reduced.

findings.description: Reading order for Table 2 differs significantly between PDF and HTML. In the PDF, a reader would see left column categories then right column categories on the same visual level. In HTML, all left-side categories appear first, then all right-side categories follow sequentially.
findings.evidence: PDF: Apparel Group and Lumber & Building Material Group are at similar vertical positions. HTML: Lumber & Building Material Group appears much later after all left-column groups are listed.

findings.description: The title and header text formatting differs slightly. PDF uses centered, bold title text. HTML uses left-aligned or differently styled header text.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'MONTHLY REPORT OF STATE SALES AND USE TAX GROSS COLLECTIONS AND TAXABLE SALES' is centered and bold. HTML: Same text appears with different alignment/weight.

findings.description: The footnotes at the bottom of page 1 and page 3 are preserved in the HTML but have slightly different visual formatting and bullet/dash styling.
findings.evidence: PDF uses em-dash (—) for notes. HTML may render these differently.

findings.description: Nested <main> elements create invalid HTML structure. The outer <body> contains a <main>, and inside it another <main> is used for the table content.

The document has two h1 elements - the page header title appears as h1, and potentially the structure uses header class='page-header' with h2 for TABLE 2, which is acceptable, but two distinct heading hierarchies exist within the same page.
findings.evidence: First h1: 'MONTHLY REPORT OF STATE SALES AND USE TAX GROSS COLLECTIONS AND TAXABLE SALES' - only one h1 is ideal but this matches the document title

findings.description: Table 1 layout uses empty rows for Motor Vehicle Lease (Short-Term) and Subscription which are split across two rows in the right-hand columns, creating slightly awkward alignment but data values are preserved correctly.
findings.evidence: Hyde row has 'Motor Vehicle Lease (Short-Term)' with empty collections/sales, and Iredell row has 'and Subscription' with 9,173,947 and '–'. All values match the PDF.
